assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and email address.
The developer has a GitHub account and an accessible repository containing an existing server-side web application.
The application already binds its HTTP server to 0.0.0.0 and has known build and start commands.

developer_goal: Create a Render account, deploy an existing server-side application from GitHub through the Render Dashboard, and open the live application.

first_success.outcome: The web service is Live and its onrender.com URL serves the application's root content.
first_success.observable_signal: Opening the service's onrender.com URL returns the application's root content after the deploy reaches Live.

route_selection.rule: Use the shortest complete Dashboard path. Connect GitHub inside the service creation flow instead of taking the separate Account Settings detour.
route_selection.selected: Dashboard Web Service creation with in-flow GitHub connection

candidates.name: Dashboard with in-flow GitHub connection
candidates.reason: Render documents that an unconnected Git provider can be connected directly from the service creation form.

candidates.name: Dashboard with Account Settings Git credential setup first
candidates.reason: It reaches the same Dashboard deployment outcome but adds a separate navigation detour.

candidates.name: Render CLI or coding-agent deployment
candidates.reason: These are different interaction surfaces from the requested account-creation-to-Dashboard-success path.

required_path.action: Create a Render account with the email and password route.

required_fields.label: Terms of Service and Privacy Policy acknowledgement
required_fields.notes: The signup page states that creating the account constitutes agreement; there is no separate visible checkbox.

required_fields.label: hCaptcha challenge
required_fields.notes: The challenge may be passive or interactive depending on risk signals.

required_path.observable_result: The developer has an authenticated Render Dashboard account.

required_path.action: In the Render Dashboard, choose New > Web Service.
required_path.observable_result: The service creation flow opens with source options.

required_path.action: Select Git Provider and choose GitHub from the service creation flow.
required_path.observable_result: Render redirects the developer to GitHub authorization.

required_path.action: Authorize Render's GitHub integration to access the repository used for the deployment.

required_fields.label: Repository access
required_fields.notes: Access must include the repository being deployed.

required_path.observable_result: GitHub redirects back to Render and the repository list is available.

required_path.action: Select the application repository and choose Connect.
required_path.observable_result: The remainder of the Web Service creation form appears.

required_path.action: Complete the required Web Service configuration.
required_path.observable_result: The form contains the source, runtime, build, start, location, name, and instance settings required to create the service.

required_path.action: Select Deploy or Create Web Service to submit the service configuration.
required_path.observable_result: Render creates the service and starts its first build and deploy.

required_path.action: After the deploy reaches Live, open the service's onrender.com URL.
required_path.observable_result: The browser displays the deployed application's root content.

prerequisites.description: A GitHub account with access to a repository containing the server-side application.
The application listens for HTTP requests on 0.0.0.0 and has known build and start commands.

external_gates.description: GitHub authorization must grant Render access to the selected repository.

unavoidable_waits.description: Render must clone, build, start, and mark the first deploy Live before the application URL can be verified.

platform_outcomes.description: Render creates the Web Service and starts the first deploy after submission.
A successful deploy becomes Live and receives an onrender.com URL.
The service returns the application's root content when the URL is opened.

excluded.reason: Documentation is evidence for the path, not a product action.

excluded.item: Connecting GitHub through Account Settings before creating the service
excluded.reason: Render documents an in-flow connection from the service creation form, which removes the detour.
Required only for a monorepo, which is not assumed by this selected route.
They are conditional on the application and are not required by the selected example route.
The developer may watch logs, but no interaction is required while a successful deploy runs.
